name: DummyJSON
description: Free public REST API with realistic fake e-commerce data (products, carts, users, posts, comments, quotes, recipes). Supports pagination, filtering, sorting, and simulated auth flows. Good fit for prototyping listing/detail UI flows or rate-limit + pagination harness tests.

auth.instructions: Read endpoints require no authentication. A simulated /auth/login flow returns a JWT for testing bearer-token wiring, but no endpoint enforces the token in production-relevant ways.

endpoints.description: List products with pagination. Query: limit, skip, select, sortBy, order.
List 100 fake users (name, address, company, bank, crypto wallet).
List 150 fake posts with tags + reactions.
Simulated JWT issue. Body: { username, password }. Returns token but no endpoint truly enforces it.

guidelines: Use `limit` + `skip` for pagination; default page size is 30 items.
Use `select=field1,field2` to keep payloads small — full product objects can be 1-2 KB each.
For schema-validation tests, the response shapes are stable: `products` returns `{products, total, skip, limit}`.
Use this when you need a more 'shaped' fake than jsonplaceholder (which only has flat posts/users/todos).
avoid: Don't treat the /auth/login JWT as a real security boundary — it is for client-side wiring tests, not server-side auth simulation.
Don't rely on POST/PUT/DELETE persistence — those endpoints echo back the body but no state is stored across requests.
notes: Maintained as an open project on GitHub: https://github.com/Ovi/DummyJSON.
